providerStateTaxonomy.meaning: Default safe state. Analyzer provider execution is unavailable and analyze-request must block unless an explicit external candidate is imported.
Provider name, model name, and environment variable references may be present for future review, but this state still grants no invocation authority in the current implementation.

Future explicit invocation candidate state. It records that a provider config could be eligible for a future adapter only when a separate --invoke-provider flag is explicitly provided. This state grants no current invocation authority.

OpenAI live-provider-specific config shape for a future adapter. It is distinct from configured-invocation-enabled-preview and still grants no current invocation authority. Future live invocation would require --invoke-provider, --allow-network-provider, provider mode openai, an implemented adapter, and runtime secret handling that stores no secret values.
A provider configuration was expected but a required non-secret reference or local configuration source is missing or unreadable.
The configuration contains an unsafe or unknown provider state, a secret-looking value, a disallowed model/provenance field, or an authority escalation flag.
Vocabulary reserved for a future provider-adapter decision. It is not active in this boundary and cannot be used by current artifacts to make network calls.

secretPolicy.secretRedactionRequirement: Future tools may record only the reference name and must never store or print secret values.

providerProvenancePolicy.providerConfiguredMeaning: configured means references are present for review only; it does not mean invocation is allowed

futureInvocationEnablementRequirements: external candidate import must not be supplied in the same command
provider adapter implementation must exist in a later slice
futureOpenAiLiveInvocationEnablementRequ: external candidate import and mock provider response input must not be supplied in the same command
OpenAI adapter and SDK dependency must exist in a later slice
API key value may be read only at runtime from apiKeySourceRef and must not be stored, printed, or committed
futureAnalyzeRequestIntegration.configuredProviderCurrentMeaning: configured-not-invoked must still return no network call and no candidate generation until a future provider adapter is explicitly implemented
futureAnalyzeRequestIntegration.invocationEnabledProviderCurrentMeaning: configured-invocation-enabled-preview is a boundary-only future state and still grants no current invocation authority

forbiddenUse: store API key, token, or secret values in provider config artifacts
treat the future --invoke-provider flag as implemented in this slice
treat the future --allow-network-provider flag as implemented in this slice
treat the future --provider-mode openai flag as implemented in this slice
generate Request IR Candidate JSON from provider config alone
skip analyze-request provider-disabled behavior without a future explicit provider adapter
drive graph validation or traversal from provider config
generate selected graph slices, contract input, or instruction packs from provider config

runtimeBudgetBoundary.reason: This boundary preview adds no command and no provider invocation.

nonExecutionBoundary: This artifact previews the AI Request Analyzer provider configuration boundary only. It does not implement a provider config reader, call an LLM/API, make network calls, generate a Request IR Candidate, validate Request IR, run graph traversal, generate selected graph slices, generate Contract Compiler Input, generate Instruction Packs, execute Codex, install or run hooks, mutate graph-source, apply graph deltas, automate approval or human decisions, satisfy runtime Evidence, accept Evidence, prove equivalence, enforce scope, configure CI, activate strict/guided blocking, or grant Project Memory extension authority.
